data.title: Morgan Stanley Q4 earnings, revenue top, helped by investment banking, wealth management
data.description:
Morgan Stanley (MS) Q4 earnings beat the average analyst estimate, helped by solid revenue growth from its wealth management and investment management units, both from the prior and year-ago quarters. In institutional securities, investment banking revenue jumped 47% Y/Y.

Q4 GAAP EPS of $2.68, topping the average analyst estimate of $2.42, fell from $2.80 in Q3 and rose from $2.22 in Q4 2024.

Net revenue of $17.9B, exceeding the $17.8B consensus, dropped from $18.2B in the prior quarter and grew from $16.2B a year ago.

Morgan Stanley’s (MS) net interest income of $2.86B, vs. the Visible Alpha consensus of $2.53B, increased from $2.49B in Q3 and $2.55B in the prior year’s Q4.

Pretax profit margin of 32% vs. 33% in the previous quarter and 30% a year ago. Compensation and benefits, as a percentage of net revenue, were 39%, compared with 41% in Q3 and 39% in the year-ago Q4.

Total non-compensation expenses were $5.05B vs. $4.75B in the prior quarter and $4.91B a year ago.

Provision for credit losses of $18M, vs. $88.3M Visible Alpha consensus, rose from $0M in the previous quarter and fell from $115M a year ago.

Return on average tangible common equity of 21.8% vs. 23.5% in Q3 and 20.2% in Q4 2024.

Morgan Stanley (MS) stock edged up 0.1% in Thursday premarket trading.

Revenue by segment:

  • Institutional Securities: $7.93B dipped 7% Q/Q and increased 9% Y/Y; investment banking revenue of $2.41B increased 14% Q/Q and 47% Y/Y. Equity net revenue of $3.67B dropped 11% Q/Q and rose 10% Y/Y, and fixed income net revenue of $1.76B fell 19% Q/Q and 9% Y/Y.

  • Wealth Management: $8.43B, up 2% Q/Q and 13% Y/Y. Asset management revenue of $5.03B climbed 5% Q/Q and 14% Y/Y. Total client assets of $7.38T grew 5% Q/Q and 19% Y/Y.

  • Investment Management: $1.72B grew 4% Q/Q and 5% Y/Y. Asset management and related fees of $1.65B rose 7% Q/Q and 6% Y/Y. Long-term net inflows were $1.7B in Q4 2025, down from $16.5B in Q3 and $4.3B in Q4 2024. Long-term assets under management or supervision of $1.25T rose 1% Q/Q and 14% Y/Y.

Conference call at 8:30 AM ET.

Earlier, Morgan Stanley GAAP EPS of $2.68 beats by $0.23, revenue of $17.89B beats by $140M

data.title: UGI to sell Eastern Europe LPG businesses in €48M deal
data.description:
UGI International (UGI) said Thursday it agreed to sell its liquefied petroleum gas distribution businesses in Czech Republic, Hungary, Poland, and Slovakia, to DCC plc for an enterprise value of ~€48M.

"This divestiture substantially completes UGI International’s previously announced portfolio optimization program, allowing us to sharpen our focus on the segments where we have the strongest competitive positions and growth opportunities," UGI International (UGI) President Julie Fazio said.

UGI International, a subsidiary of UGI Corp. (UGI), is a leading LPG distributor operating in 14 European countries, selling 820M gallons of LPG in 2025 to customers throughout Europe.

data.title: Verano expands revolving credit facility to $100M, extends maturity
data.description:
• Verano Holdings (VRNO:CA) has amended its revolving credit facility, increasing commitments from $75M to $100M and extending maturity to February 28, 2029.
  • The upsized facility required no additional collateral and remains secured by certain owned real estate.
  • $50M has been drawn to date, leaving $50M available subject to certain conditions.
  • The amendment enhances Verano’s balance sheet flexibility, supporting lower-cost debt, redraw optionality, and potential release of real estate collateral.

data.title: Morgan Stanley in charts: Wealth Management revenue rises 12.7% Y/Y
Oshkosh Defense wins $53M U.S. army order for Common Bridge Transporters
data.description:
• Oshkosh Defense (OSK) has received a $53M order from U.S. Army Contracting Command–Detroit Arsenal for Common Bridge Transporters under the Family of Heavy Tactical Vehicle V program.

  • The Common Bridge Transporter is built on Oshkosh’s heavy tactical truck platform and is designed to support combat mobility in complex terrain and threat environments.

  • The vehicle enables the transport, launch, and retrieval of bridge bays and bridge erection boats to help maneuver forces cross wet gaps.

  • The order reinforces Oshkosh Defense’s role as a key supplier of advanced mobility solutions to the U.S. Army.

data.title: London Stock Exchange launches digital settlement service that expands tokenised cash, cash-like solutions in market
data.description:
London Stock Exchange Group (LSEGY) (LDNXF) on Thursday announced the launch of a new digital settlement service, Digital Settlement House.

"LSEG DiSH expands the tokenised cash and cash-like solutions available to the market and, for the first time, offers a real cash solution tokenised on the blockchain utilizing cash in multiple currencies held at commercial banks," said Daniel Maguire, group head, LSEG Markets and CEO, LCH Group.

"This innovative service will enable users to reduce settlement risk, and integrate existing cash, securities, and digital assets across new and existing market infrastructure," added Maguire.

Digital Settlement House enables programmatic and instantaneous settlement between independent payment networks, both on and off chain.

Market participants are expected to be able to conduct PvP or DvP and settlements using any asset, orchestrating payments on any connected network, digital and traditional.

LSEG DiSH is set to operate through the stock exchange's Post Trade Solutions business.

data.title: Ocular surges on report of Sanofi buyout bid
data.description:
Ocular Therapeutix (OCUL) added ~21% in the premarket on Thursday after the French publication La Lettre reported that Sanofi (SNY) is readying an improved takeover offer to acquire the U.S. eye therapy developer.

Since last autumn, Sanofi (SNY) CEO Paul Hudson and his management team have been working on an enhanced bid after the Bedford, Massachusetts-based biopharma rejected the French pharma giant’s prior offer of $16 per share in September.

Sanofi (SNY) made the previous “discreet” approach to Ocular (OCUL) when the latter was trading at  $10.50 per share on the Nasdaq, indicating a market capitalization of approximately $2.2B.

A potential acquisition of Ocular (OCUL) is expected to give the Paris-based pharma giant a beachhead in wet age-related macular degeneration ((wet AMD)), the most common cause of severe vision loss among older adults.

Ocular's (OCUL) tyrosine kinase inhibitor Axpaxli is currently undergoing late-stage development for wet AMD across two studies called SOL-1 and SOL-R, which are scheduled to report topline data in Q1 2026 and H1 2027, respectively. 

At a time when its blockbuster asthma drug Dupixent is facing loss of market exclusivity over the coming years, Sanofi (SNY) has ramped up its M&A spree. In December, the company agreed to acquire Hep B vaccine maker Dynavax Technologies (DVAX) for nearly $2.2B, a transaction expected to close in Q1 2026.

data.title: Pantheon Resources raises $10M to advance Alaska North Slope projects
data.description:
• Pantheon Resources (PTHRF) has raised $10M (before expenses) through a conditional placing of new shares at 7.0 pence per share to fund near-term appraisal activities and working capital.
  • The funding will support the resumption of Dubhe-1 flow testing, including data acquisition, analysis, and preparation for a cost-effective restart.
  • The programme also supports the broader Greater Ahpun Area, which hosts over 500M barrels of estimated 2C contingent resources.
  • Additional proceeds will be used to reprocess existing Kodiak seismic data to improve reservoir imaging and support a potential appraisal well in the 2026/27 winter, subject to further financing.

data.title: Wells Fargo likes DraftKings over Flutter Entertainment into earnings season
data.description:
Wells Fargo flipped ratings on DraftKings (DKNG) and Flutter Entertainment (FLUT) on Thursday as it sees better near-term upside for DraftKings (DKNG) on the expectation of a strong Q4 earnings report. 

Analyst Trey Bowers and his team expect DraftKings (DKNG) to exceed the implied high-end of its Q4 guidance of $273M. By contrast, Flutter Entertainment's (FLUT) lower handle growth and higher promotional activity are expected to lead to an earnings disappointment. 

Following the ratings reset, DraftKings (DKNG) is now slotted at Overweight at Wells Fargo, while Flutter Entertainment (FLUT) was dropped to Equalweight. Wells Fargo's price target on DKNG of $49 represents 40% upside potential. The price target of $228 on FLUT represents 12% upside potential.

Shares of DraftKings (DKNG) were up 1.3% in premarket trading to $34.77. Flutter Entertainment (FLUT) dipped 0.3% to $199.85.

data.title: Goldman Sachs Q4 earnings top consensus; revenue misses due to Apple Card markdown
data.description:
Goldman Sachs (NYSE:GS) Q4 earnings beat the consensus estimate, but its revenue missed Wall Street expectations due to markdowns related to its pending sale of the Apple (AAPL) credit card portfolio to JPMorgan Chase (JPM).

Q4 GAAP EPS of $14.01, topping the average analyst estimate of $11.65, rose from $12.25 in Q3 and $11.95 in Q4 2024. 

Total revenue of $13.5B, lagging the $13.9B consensus, declined from $15.2B in the prior quarter and $13.9B a year ago. The decline reflected the negative net revenue in Platform Solutions, which took a $2.26B hit from markdowns related to the Apple Card portfolio. That amount, though, was more than offset by a reserve reduction of $2.48B in provision for credit losses, also related to the Apple Card transaction.

Q4 net interest income of $3.71B, exceeding the Visible Alpha consensus of $2.75B, climbed from $3.85B in Q3 and $2.35B in Q4 a year ago.

Provision for credit losses was a benefit of $2.12B, higher than the Visible Alpha consensus of a $663M benefit. That compared with a provision of $339M in the previous quarter and $351M a year ago. The net benefit for Q4 2025 reflected a net release related to the Apple Card portfolio, including a reserve reduction of $2.48B related to the transfer of Apple Card loans held for sale.

Total operating expenses of $9.72B, compared with the Visible Alpha estimate of $9.45B, rose from $9.45B in Q3 and $8.26B in Q4 2024.

Annualized return on average common shareholders' equity was 16.0%, up 1.8 percentage points from the prior quarter and 1.4 pp from the year-ago quarter.

Loans of $238B at Dec. 31, 2025, grew from $222B at Sept. 30, 2025. Deposits of $501B increased from $490B at the end of Q3.

Headcount totaled 47,400, down from 48,300 at the end of Q3. 

Goldman Sachs (GS) stock dipped 0.6% in Thursday premarket trading.

Global Banking & Market net revenue of $10.4B increased 2% Q/Q and 22% Y/Y. Investment banking fees of $2.58B slipped 3% Q/Q and climbed 25% Y/Y. FICC net revenue of $3.11B dropped 11% Q/Q and rose 12% Y/Y. Equity revenue of $4.31B grew 15% Q/Q and 25% Y/Y.

Asset & Wealth Management net revenue of $4.72B rose 7% Q/Q and edged down 1% Y/Y; management and other fees totaled $3.09B, increasing 5% Q/Q and 10% Y/Y.

Platform Solutions net revenue of -$1.68B swings from $598M in Q3 and $592M in the year-ago quarter.

Conference call at 9:30 AM ET.
